title: [BUG] Error opening Excel file containing table after opening and saving with ExcelJS
author: protobi-pieter
body: ## 🐛 Bug Report Using ExcelJS to open and save a simple Excel file, Lib version: X.Y.Z ## Steps To Reproduce ### Steps - Use ExcelJS to open the attached workbook - Use ExcelJS to save as a new workbook with no changes - Open the saved workbook in Excel - Observe that Excel complains - "Alert: We found a problem with some content in 'Microsoft_Excel_Worksheet.resaved.xlsx'. Do you want us to try to recover as much as we can? If you trust the source of this workbook, click Yes." ### Input file [Microsoft_Excel_Worksheet.xlsx](https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs/files/13312004/Microsoft_Excel_Worksheet.xlsx) - this is a simple file with 5 rows and 4 columns - it was created by Microsoft PowerPoint, - Specifically it is the initial embedded worksheet created when you insert a new column chart within PowerPoint UI ### Node.js code ```js var fs = require("fs"); var path = require('path') let ExcelJS = require('exceljs') var INFILE = './ppt/embeddings/Microsoft_Excel_Worksheet.xlsx' var OUTFILE = '../updated/Microsoft_Excel_Worksheet.resaved.xlsx'; let test = { run: async function() { var filepath = path.join(__dirname, INFILE) let xlsxContent = await fs.promises.readFile(filepath) const workbook = new ExcelJS.Workbook(); await workbook.xlsx.load(xlsxContent) const fileBuffer = await workbook.xlsx.writeBuffer(); await fs.promises.writeFile(OUTFILE, fileBuffer) console.log(`open "${OUTFILE}"`) } } test.run().then(process.exit) ``` ### Error message ### Recovery log ```xml Repair Result to Microsoft_Excel_Worksheet.resaved0.xml Errors were detected in file './Microsoft_Excel_Worksheet.resaved.xlsx' Removed Feature: AutoFilter from /xl/tables/table1.xml part (Table) Removed Feature: Table from /xl/tables/table1.xml part (Table) ``` ## The expected behaviour: The file should open without error, and look like the original workbook ## Possible solution (optional, but very helpful): **Update** This may be a duplicate of https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs/issues/2277 It turns that using this repo @nbelyh/exceljs mentioned in #2184 appears to work and also allow data values to update. But if I change any of the column names in the table the error will appear again even in this fork. But if I convert the table to a range in the original file, I can modify all cells in the range and it works ok. So the branch @nbelyh/exceljs will preserve tables but doesn't yet allow updates to the column names (at least not just be simply using `cell.value = "Series X"` to change the value) ~Preserve tables and autofilters on open and save~ ~Or allow an option to remove them, as in my specific case I don't need them.~

comments.author: cgoguyer
comments.body: Same issue, is there any news on that bug ?

comments.author: hyperliskdev
comments.body:
Figured this out, Its because of how the autofilter feature is read and written by ExcelJS. When excel reads the autofilter features, it is expecting something that looks like this `<autoFilter ref="A1:B5" xr:uid="{Random_UUID}"/>` but ExcelJS has an extra inner tag called `filterColumn` and it looks like this;
```
<autoFilter ref="A1:B5">
    <filterColumn colId="0" hiddenButton="1"/>
    <filterColumn colId="1" hiddenButton="1"/>
</autoFilter>
```
To solve this issue, the autoFilter XForm reading and writing needs to change to include the uid and the tag must be self closing.

comments.author: protobi-pieter
comments.body: @hyperliskdev  Thanks! If the tag is self-closing, how would Excel save the state of the autofilter indicating which columns are sorted, filtere, etc.?   Is the issue simply that there needs to be an `xr:uid="..."` attribute on the `<autoFilter>` tag?

comments.author: hyperliskdev
comments.body: I believe the auto filter applies to all columns. I haven't checked out how regular filters work though. I have some screenshots I can put here but it'll have to be tomorrow as I'm away from my computer now.

comments.author: hyperliskdev
comments.body:
This is a screenshot of a workbook after excel has fixed it. 
![image](https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs/assets/32578619/a9fa6e04-1a7b-4a30-bc75-849faf9246fe)

This is what ExcelJS does to the workbook file after simply using the `workbook.xlsx.load()` function. 
![image](https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs/assets/32578619/3299a133-a17e-46bb-97f8-9e04904df0d1)

Finally, checking a normal excel file created in excel, it looks like the filtered values appear like this. It looks like it has a self-closing `<filter/>` tag for each value that does appear in the sheet. The filter applied to this table just hides one value from the zeroth column
![image](https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs/assets/32578619/430e3567-de2e-4e2a-97a6-9d3cb4564e87)


It looks like the autoFilter tags are more of a static thing rather than containing any proper information for forming a filter, excel just does the calculations inside excel then gives the results to the workbook rather than giving them any specific formula or identifiers. just straight up values.

comments.author: hyperliskdev
comments.body:
![image](https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs/assets/32578619/c86ae223-2915-4a23-a9c4-e67ddcc081ea)
This is the table structure for a sorted table. This is sorted in acending numerical order.

comments.author: hyperliskdev
comments.body:
![image](https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs/assets/32578619/e0ab5cac-6496-4f67-ade5-e34c8f3fd414)
Here is a screenshot from the OpenXML Standards document, the way that filter columns appear here is different than the filterXForm and customFilterXForm

comments.author: hyperliskdev
comments.body:
https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs/blob/ac96f9a61e9799c7776bd940f05c4a51d7200209/lib/xlsx/xform/table/filter-column-xform.js#L35-L52

Here, the render may be written correctly but this is where the problem is.
https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs/blob/ac96f9a61e9799c7776bd940f05c4a51d7200209/lib/xlsx/xform/table/filter-column-xform.js#L54-L74

the FilterXForm is never rendered so the filterColumn is automatically closed when a filterColumn. I will be testing this render method today.

` this.map.filters.render(xmlStream, model.filters); `

comments.author: hyperliskdev
comments.body: @pietersv I have a "working" branch in my fork of ExcelJS. It does not make the sheet readonly when opened and saved. There is more work to be done because a test I performed with the `xmlStream.leafNode()` function actually didnt work so Ill have to look more into the standard to see why saving the sheet with an autofilter fails. At this point, the AutoFilter function is completely disabled which does work for me right now but in the near future I would like to fix this part.
